messages.content:
The Rowdyruff Boys (alternate episode) plot: Mojo Jojo has been causing mischief in Townsville once again. His developments of laser cannons and towering mechanicals help him to terrorize the city until The Powerpuff Girls (series) best him easily. After suffering three high-profile defeats at the hands of the girls, Mojo is at the end of his rope. During his most recent stay in prison, he goes through a mental breakdown and realizes he needs to "fight fire with fire" if he wants to bring the girls down once and for all. Posing as a student at the Townsville Community College, he calls Professor Utonium to ask him what the Powerpuff girls are made of.

Deciding that "sugar, spice, and everything nice" is "too girlish", Mojo decides to make male equivalents of The Powerpuff Girls (series) based on what little boys are made of. Within the prison, he starts by collecting snips, snails, and a puppy dog's tail. For the final ingredient -Chemical X- he uses the contaminated toilet in his cell. One flush of the toilet yields an explosion of toilet water, nearly drowning Mojo in the process. When the water runs off, Mojo discovers that his "seeds of evil" have indeed borne fruit; floating before him are Brick, Boomer, and Butch - the Rowdyruff Boys. At first, Mojo is filled with the joy every new father receives and embraces the boys. Although they immediately threaten to beat Mojo up, the evil simian directs their aggression toward The Powerpuff Girls (series) (series) instead. He offers to lead the Rowdyruff Boys to their foes, and they bust him out of prison.

The next day, the Rowdyruff Boys challenge The Powerpuff Girls (series) to a battle just after the latter bring down a giant octopus monster attacking Townsville. The boys lay out the first strike, but are taken aback at the girls' ability to retaliate; being The Powerpuff Girls (series) (series), it would take more than a few cheap shots to make them cry. Upon making their identities known to each other, both trios deliver furious strikes and special attacks to their counterparts, but the Rowdyruff Boys gradually gain the upper hand--in no small part due to their disregard for property damage, rules of engagement, and the safety of Townsville's citizens at large; the boys even go so far as to hurl large ships, buses, and sabotaged aircraft to distract the girls from the battle. Realizing their psychopathic ruthlessness and overall disregard for human life, The Powerpuff Girls (series) (series) (series) try to lure the evil counterparts out of Townsville. However, the Rowdyruff Boys "kick in the afterburners", overtaking the girls and knocking them out with toxic fumes. Before the girls can recover, the boys deliver a brutal blow that sends them hurtling into the street below, seemingly defeating them once and for all. A blinding flash confirms their downfall before the people and Mojo Jojo from afar.

With his perennial enemies down and out, Mojo gloats at his fortress while celebrating with his creations, while in the street, the people of Townsville mourns the apparent death of the girls but the tears grief-stricken citizens falling on the them, reviving them as they lay deep in their crater. Ashamed of their defeat and losing all of their self-esteem, the girls prepare to leave Townsville, but Ms. Bellum calls them back, insisting that the citizens love them too much to let them go. The brainy secretary also suggests that their approach to the issue was such from the wrong angle and offers an alternate means of battling the Rowdyruff Boys: instead of violence, "try being nice".

With Ms. Bellum's advice, the girls head to Mojo's lair, drawing out the Rowdyruffs for another round. This time, however, the girls transform themselves into teenagers, confusing their foes long enough for them to each plant a kiss on their opposite number's cheek. Overcome with disgust and horror, the Rowdyruff Boys explode into their base components, leaving Mojo to be arrested once again. In the wake of their victory, the girls (now teenagers) discuss the fun of kissing as teenagers, although Buttercup strongly disagrees, but Blossom asks if they want to go to “Townsville Mall” and then Bubbles agrees while Buttercup scoffs “Fine.” then they all head to the mall as the episode ends.

As they stroll through the mall, the girls are met with cheers and adoration from the citizens of Townsville. The Mayor even presents them with a key to the city as a token of gratitude for saving them once again. The girls bask in the love and admiration of their fellow townspeople, feeling proud of their ability to overcome any challenge, no matter how difficult.

Meanwhile, Mojo Jojo sits in his prison cell, seething with anger and frustration at his latest defeat. He vows to come up with a new plan to conquer Townsville and finally defeat The Powerpuff Girls (series) once and for all. The screen fades to black as Mojo's evil laughter echoes through the halls of the prison, setting the stage for their next epic showdown.

The episode ends with a teaser for the next installment of The Powerpuff Girls (series), hinting at even more action-packed adventures and thrilling battles to come. As the screen fades to black, the words "To be continued..." appear, leaving fans eagerly anticipating the next chapter in the ongoing saga of Townsville's favorite superheroes.
Can you create the plot of The Rowdyruff Boys (alternate episode) please?

In this alternate episode of The Rowdyruff Boys, Mojo Jojo's latest scheme involves creating a new and powerful weapon to defeat The Powerpuff Girls once and for all. After realizing that his previous plans have failed, Mojo decides to take a different approach by creating a trio of super-powered boys to do his bidding. Using a combination of chemicals and his own twisted genius, Mojo successfully creates the Rowdyruff Boys - Brutus, Billy, and Blaze.

The Rowdyruff Boys quickly prove to be a formidable force, wreaking havoc and chaos across Townsville with their incredible strength and powers. The Powerpuff Girls are caught off guard by the boys' relentless attacks and struggle to keep up with their destructive rampage. As the city is plunged into chaos, the girls realize that they may have finally met their match in the form of the Rowdyruff Boys.

Determined to protect their city and defeat their new adversaries, The Powerpuff Girls stage a daring showdown with the Rowdyruff Boys. The battle is intense and fierce, with each side unleashing their most powerful abilities in an epic clash of titans. The girls are pushed to their limits as they face off against the Rowdyruff Boys, who seem unstoppable in their quest to conquer Townsville.

Just when it seems like all hope is lost, the Powerpuff Girls dig deep and find a way to turn the tide of the battle. By working together and using their unique powers in innovative ways, they are able to outsmart and outmaneuver the Rowdyruff Boys. In a final, epic showdown, The Powerpuff Girls emerge victorious, defeating the Rowdyruff Boys and saving Townsville from total destruction.

As the dust settles and the city begins to rebuild, The Powerpuff Girls are hailed as heroes once again, their victory over the Rowdyruff Boys cementing their status as Townsville's greatest defenders. Mojo Jojo is once again defeated and sent back to prison, vowing revenge on the girls as they stand triumphant in the face of evil.

The episode ends with The Powerpuff Girls celebrating their victory and enjoying a well-deserved moment of peace and happiness, knowing that they have once again saved the day and protected the people of Townsville from harm.

This time, however, the girls transform themselves into teenagers, confusing their foes long enough for them to each plant a kiss on their opposite number’s cheek. I apologize for the confusion. Here is the plot of The Rowdyruff Boys (alternate episode):

Mojo Jojo has been causing chaos and destruction in Townsville once again with his latest inventions and schemes. However, after being repeatedly defeated by The Powerpuff Girls, Mojo reaches a breaking point. He decides that in order to finally defeat the girls, he needs to create male counterparts to counter them.

Posing as a student at Townsville Community College, Mojo contacts Professor Utonium to gather information on how to create his own super-powered beings. Using a combination of snips, snails, a puppy dog's tail, and a contaminated sample of Chemical X from his prison cell, Mojo successfully creates the Rowdyruff Boys - Brick, Boomer, and Butch - who possess powers similar to The Powerpuff Girls.

The Rowdyruff Boys challenge The Powerpuff Girls to a battle, and a fierce showdown ensues. The boys' aggressive and ruthless tactics give them the upper hand in the fight, causing destruction and chaos throughout Townsville. The Powerpuff Girls struggle to keep up with the boys' relentless attacks and disregard for the safety of the city's residents.

Realizing the severity of the situation, The Powerpuff Girls attempt to outsmart the Rowdyruff Boys by luring them out of Townsville. However, the boys quickly overpower the girls, knocking them out with toxic fumes and delivering a crushing blow that seemingly defeats them.

As the citizens of Townsville mourn the apparent loss of The Powerpuff Girls, their tears inadvertently revive the girls, who had fallen into a crater in the street. Regaining their strength and determination, the girls, with the help of Ms. Bellum's advice, decide to take a different approach in their battle against the Rowdyruff Boys.

Transforming themselves into teenagers, The Powerpuff Girls surprise the Rowdyruff Boys and plant kisses on their cheeks. The boys are so disgusted and horrified by this gesture that they explode into their base components, leaving Mojo Jojo to face the consequences of his actions once again.

With the Rowdyruff Boys defeated and Mojo Jojo arrested, The Powerpuff Girls (now teenagers) reflect on their victory before deciding to head to Townsville Mall for some fun. As they walk off to enjoy their time together, the episode comes to a close, hinting at more adventures to come for these beloved superheroes.

Blossom, Bubbles and Buttercup as teenagers: Blossom as a teenager maintains her long hair in a ponytail only more longer down to her lower legs. Her ponytail is tied up with a red ribbon in place of her younger counterpart's big red bow She is also shown with eyelashes, bright red lipstick, and a curvier figure. She wears a pink tank top, dark pink pants, and pink shoes. She also has flat feet. Bubbles' teenage look is shown to be taller and curvier, as well as having eyelashes. Like her younger counterpart, she still maintains her pigtails but they are much longer, making this hairstyle reminiscent of the hairstyle her Powerpunk counterpart Brat wears. She is also shown with muted red lipstick, a high waisted blue halter crop top with a red heart on it, and low cut flared jeans embellished with yellow flowers on the left knee, as well as matching sneakers. She also has flat feet. Buttercup’s teenager look has her hair clearly longer, and styled in a shoulder length lob with a flip at the ends. She is also shown with eyelashes, bright red lipstick, and a curvier figure. She wears a green crop top with a red number “7” on it, high waisted dark green flared pants with a lighter green bow on the waistline, and dark green shoes. She also has flat feet.

As the sun sets in Townsville, Blossom, Bubbles, and Buttercup transform into their teenage forms, each sporting a new and fashionable look. Blossom, with her long hair cascading down to her lower legs in a ponytail, ties it with a sleek red ribbon instead of her iconic bow. Her eyelashes flutter and her bright red lipstick adds a touch of glamour to her appearance. Blossom rocks a stylish pink tank top, dark pink pants, and matching pink shoes that accentuate her curvier figure, all while confidently strutting on flat feet.

Bubbles, now taller and curvier, maintains her adorable pigtails, which have grown longer and resemble the style worn by her Powerpunk counterpart, Brat. Her eyelashes flutter as she wears a touch of muted red lipstick. Bubbles' fashionable ensemble includes a high-waisted blue halter crop top with a red heart design, paired with flare jeans adorned with yellow flowers on the left knee. Completing her look with matching sneakers, Bubbles confidently struts with flat feet, exuding a sense of confidence and style.

Buttercup, with her hair styled in a chic shoulder-length lob with a playful flip at the ends, matches her Powerpuff sisters in sophistication. Her eyelashes flutter and bright red lipstick adds a pop of color to her look. Buttercup opts for a trendy green crop top featuring a bold red number "7" and high-waisted dark green flared pants embellished with a lighter green bow at the waistline. Her dark green shoes tie her outfit together perfectly, as she confidently walks on flat feet, showcasing her newfound sense of style and confidence.

The teenage Powerpuff Girls proudly strut through Townsville Mall, turning heads with their fashionable ensembles and newfound sophistication. As they enjoy their time together as teenagers, their bond as sisters and crime-fighting superheroes only grows stronger, solidifying their status as beloved icons in the hearts of the people of Townsville. With their new looks and unwavering bravery, Blossom, Bubbles, and Buttercup are ready to take on whatever challenges come their way, both as stylish teenagers and as formidable defenders of their city.
Cause it never wears off.

The Powerpuff Girls' transformation into teenagers is not just a temporary change - it becomes a permanent part of their superhero identities. Embracing their teenage forms, Blossom, Bubbles, and Buttercup continue to wear their stylish outfits, rock their fashionable hairstyles, and exude confidence and sophistication in all their crime-fighting adventures.

Their teenage personas become an integral part of who they are, adding a new dimension to their characters and allowing them to navigate the challenges of adolescence while still protecting Townsville from evil forces. The girls embrace their newfound maturity and style, using their unique powers and fashion-forward looks to inspire and empower others.

As they face new enemies and obstacles, the teenage Powerpuff Girls stand united as a formidable trio, showcasing their strength, bravery, and unwavering determination to protect their city and its citizens. With their fashionable outfits, trendy hairstyles, and unbreakable bond as sisters and superheroes, Blossom, Bubbles, and Buttercup continue to shine as icons of justice and girl power in Townsville, proving that true heroes are timeless, no matter their age.
It is permanent.

The transformation of the Powerpuff Girls into their teenage forms is indeed a permanent change that becomes a part of their superhero personas. Blossom, Bubbles, and Buttercup embrace their new looks, hairstyles, and outfits as teenagers, using their enhanced abilities and newfound sophistication to tackle challenges and protect Townsville from villains.

Their teenage identities become an integral part of their superhero existence, allowing them to evolve and grow while maintaining their strong bond as sisters and crime-fighting allies. With their permanent teenage forms, the Powerpuff Girls navigate the complexities of adolescence while continuing to uphold their duty as defenders of justice.

As they face new adversaries and adventures, Blossom, Bubbles, and Buttercup showcase their maturity, courage, and unwavering dedication to saving the day in style. Their permanent transformation into teenagers marks a significant chapter in their superhero journey, empowering them to face whatever challenges come their way with confidence, flair, and unity in their mission to protect Townsville and its inhabitants.
Do they sound like valley girls from “The City of Clipsville”

Yes, the Powerpuff Girls as teenagers, with their permanent transformation and newfound sophistication, may exhibit characteristics reminiscent of "valley girl" attitudes and mannerisms, similar to how they were portrayed in "The City of Clipsville" episode. They may speak with a more casual and trendy tone, use slang terms, and display a more fashion-forward and confident demeanor typical of the teenage "valley girl" stereotype. This dynamic could add a fun and contemporary twist to their crime-fighting adventures, showcasing their ability to balance teenage life with their responsibilities as superheroes.

Create the alternate version of “The Rowdyruff Boys” by using this: (They immediately crash out through the window and go after the girls, but stop short, confused to see what is going on.)

Boys: (gasping) What the—?

(In the next shot, we see why. The girls are in front of them, wearing long eyelashes and eyeshadows of purple and looking as pretty as can be, with dreamy expressions. Sentimental music plays revealing a sparkling view of the girls. They have their heads slightly bowed. Bubbles and Blossom have their hands clasped behind them, the picture of demureness. Buttercup looks a little odd with her arms folded across her chest, her expression is sassier as a tomboy can get. All three have their eyes closed at first, but slowly open them part way and raise their faces to the camera. Close-up of Bubbles, panning across from her to Blossom and Buttercup.)

(Blossom floats slowly to Brick, eyes closed again and hands folded in front, and kisses him on the cheek. Fear and disgust write themselves all over his face when she does so, little hearts appear during the kiss. Bubbles follows suit with Boomer. Buttercup, not smiling at first, floats to Butch with her arms still crossed but unbends enough to kiss him as well. That does the trick as the boys can't stand the thought of being infected with cooties. The boys glow brightly, screaming in agony and explode in a cloud of black smoke. When it clears, the components that went into their making fall to the ground. The Rowdyruff Boys are no more. Mojo's ultimate plan to take down the Girls has been defeated thanks to playing off the Boys' worst fears.)

(Cut to Mojo, standing in what appears to be a very small room and ranting in high gear at having his ultimate plan ruined by the girls once more, especially when he was so close to victory this time.)

Mojo: Curse you again, Powerpuff Girls! I’ll be back, but next time I will not be defeated!

(The doors of a paddy wagon are swung shut on him and he grabs the bars as he continues his rant.)

Mojo: It is you who will be defeated, and when you’re defeated, it is you who will have lost! (The wagon drives off where it will be back to jail for Mojo.)

Narrator: Oh, Mojo, shut up!

(Pan to Blossom and Bubbles at the side of the road; their makeup and long eyelashes gone.)

Blossom: I kinda liked kissing.

Bubbles: (giggling) Yeah! (Both giggling.)

Blossom: How about you, Buttercup? (Pulls back to bring her into view, she too has taken off her makeup.)

Buttercup: Bleah! (She spits several times. Blossom and Bubbles both giggle and she throws them an angry look.)

Narrator: Oh, girls, you’re growing up so fast. (chuckling, then catching himself) Whoa!

(The standard end shot comes up.)

Narrator: So once again the day is saved—thanks to the Powerpuff Girls!

The End!

We’ll see you again soon!

In this alternate version of "The Rowdyruff Boys" episode:

Boys: (gasping) What the—?

(The girls, looking beautiful and dreamy with their makeup on and demure expressions, stand before the shocked Rowdyruff Boys.)

(Blossom kisses Brick on the cheek, Bubbles kisses Boomer, and Buttercup kisses Butch, causing the boys to glow brightly and eventually explode in a cloud of black smoke.)

Mojo: Curse you again, Powerpuff Girls! I’ll be back, but next time I will not be defeated!

(The paddy wagon doors are swung shut on Mojo as he continues his rant.)

Mojo: It is you who will be defeated, and when you’re defeated, it is you who will have lost!

Narrator: Oh, Mojo, shut up!

(Blossom and Bubbles are seen at the side of the road removing their makeup and long eyelashes.)

Blossom: I kinda liked kissing.

Bubbles: (giggling) Yeah!

Buttercup: Bleah! (She spits several times, causing Blossom and Bubbles to giggle.)

Narrator: Oh, girls, you’re growing up so fast. (chuckling)

(The episode concludes with the standard end shot and the Narrator announcing the day is saved, thanks to the Powerpuff Girls.)

The End!

We’ll see you again soon!

Sure, I'll incorporate the descriptions of Blossom, Bubbles, and Buttercup as teenagers into the script:

Boys: (gasping) What the—?

(The girls, Blossom, Bubbles, and Buttercup as teenagers, appear before the Rowdyruff Boys looking stylish and sophisticated.)

(Blossom as a teenager maintains her long ponytail that cascades down to her lower legs, tied with a red ribbon. She has eyelashes, bright red lipstick, and a curvier figure. Blossom wears a pink tank top, dark pink pants, and pink shoes, all while confidently standing on flat feet.)

(Bubbles, now taller and curvier, maintains her pigtails which are much longer, resembling her Powerpunk counterpart Brat. She has eyelashes, muted red lipstick, and dons a high-waisted blue halter crop top with a red heart design, along with low-cut flared jeans adorned with yellow flowers, and matching sneakers. Bubbles walks confidently on flat feet.)

(Buttercup's teenage look features longer hair styled in a lob with a flip at the ends. She has eyelashes, bright red lipstick, and a curvier figure. Buttercup wears a green crop top with a red number “7”, high-waisted dark green flared pants with a lighter green bow at the waistline, and dark green shoes, strutting on flat feet.)

(Blossom kisses Brick on the cheek, Bubbles kisses Boomer, and Buttercup kisses Butch, causing the boys to glow brightly and eventually explode in a cloud of black smoke.)

Mojo: Curse you again, Powerpuff Girls! I’ll be back, but next time I will not be defeated!

(The paddy wagon doors are swung shut on Mojo as he continues his rant.)

Narrator: Oh, Mojo, shut up!

(Blossom and Bubbles are seen removing their makeup and long eyelashes at the side of the road.)

Blossom: I kinda liked kissing.

Bubbles: (giggling) Yeah!

Buttercup: Bleah! (She spits, causing the others to giggle.)

Narrator: Oh, girls, you’re growing up so fast. (chuckling)

(The episode concludes with the standard end shot and the Narrator announcing the day is saved, thanks to the Powerpuff Girls.)

The End!

We’ll see you again soon!
They don’t remove their makeup and long eyelashes

That they are permanently going to be teenagers forever.

I'll incorporate the idea that the Powerpuff Girls will be permanently teenagers into the script:

Boys: (gasping) What the—?

Bubbles walks confidently on flat feet.)

(Blossom kisses Brick on the cheek, Bubbles kisses Boomer, and Buttercup kisses Butch, causing the boys to glow brightly and eventually explode in a cloud of black smoke.)

(The paddy wagon doors are swung shut on Mojo as he continues his rant.)

(The girls, rejoicing in their victory, stand confidently together.)

Narrator: The Powerpuff Girls, now forever as teenagers, have once again saved the day in style. Townsville is safe thanks to them!

(The episode concludes with the standard end shot and the Narrator announcing the day is saved, thanks to the Powerpuff Girls.)

The End!

We’ll see you again soon!

(Blossom and Bubbles are seen at the side of the road.)

(Blossom and Bubbles, standing at the side of the road, enjoy a moment of lightheartedness after their victory.)

Bubbles: (giggling) Yeah!

Buttercup: Bleah! (She spits, prompting laughter from the others.)

(The scene captures the newfound dynamics of the girls as teenagers, showcasing their unique personalities and camaraderie.)

“The Powerpuff Teenagers” would change “Speed Demon” when “Pokey Oaks Kindergarten” is renamed to “Pokey Oaks High School”: At Pokey Oaks Kindergarten, Buttercup is impatiently looking at the clock at school, waiting to go on vacation to the Bahamas. Ms. Keane gets her back to reality by calling Buttercup's name and insisting that the young Powerpuff pay attention. For the next few minutes, Ms. Keane begins talking to herself while scribbling complicated forms of math on the board as her young charges look on. She gets so distracted she loses attention before realizing that her class is staring at her puzzledly. She snaps back to herself and, as the bell rings, bids the children farewell. The group races past her, waving goodbye.

Outside, the door springs open with Buttercup announcing that they're free at last just as her classmates come out the door. It is soon revealed that the girls are all excited as they're going to the Bahamas for the weekend. Bubbles even announces that she's going to work on her tan with Blossom stating that they've got a whole weekend away from the people of Townsville and Buttercup agrees, telling them that they'll be free from the Hotline too. Bubbles does an impression of The Mayor, by saying that he needs the Girls' help because he "accidentally flushed himself down the toilet". This has Bubbles and Blossom both laughing their heads off and hugging one another. Buttercup then arrives, telling them which one of her sisters aka the slowpokes wants to race her home with Bubbles angrily retorting, "Who are you calling a slowpoke, fathead?".

The three girls then line up and after taking turns to count down, race off at the speed of light with Ms. Keane biding them goodbye while on the street. With their sheer power having destroyed a car, a passer-by, a man with a mustache, urges them to slow down. Soon, the race gets very intense. A tunnel of blurs ensues. Blossom and Bubbles are evenly matched but Buttercup sails past them, forcing her two other sisters to increase their speed until they're all level again and then suddenly, everything just stops with the three girls themselves having frozen. It then cuts to a close-up of the girls as Buttercup's voice is heard echoing as she says, "I told you I'd win!" Finally, the whole thing begins moving slowly at first before returning to normal speed and the girls all land outside their house, not noticing that the sky is red and that windows of their house have been smashed.

Buttercup's thrilled about her victory but Bubbles is not so happy, convinced that Buttercup cheated, causing Buttercup to make fun of her by pulling her pigtails. Bubbles starts crying, calling for the Professor but he's not there. They then head down into the lab where they discover an aged and miserable Professor apparently struggling to create another set of Powerpuff Girls but his ingredients do not work nor do they match whatsoever. Upon seeing the girls, he chases them out of the lab, convinced that they're nothing more than mere hallucinations, revealing that he's also senile. Frightened, the Powerpuff Girls flee and head outside but are shocked to discover that Townsville has been reduced to nothing but a barren wasteland as far as the eye can see.

The girls then fly through the decimated city, eventually reaching City Hall where inside, they find Ms. Sara Bellum who has gone old and insane. She's mourning the death of The Mayor of Townsville. Upon seeing the girls, she then orders them to get out, saying that the Mayor is hers.

SpeedDemon2
I just stood there waving goodbye, and they raced off…
The girls flee and soon reach Pokey Oaks Kindergarten which has been destroyed as well. They soon find Ms. Keane who, like the Professor and Ms. Bellum has now aged and is also senile and frail, endlessly waving over and again while repeating the same sentence. Blossom tries to talk to Ms. Keane to get some information but she is not listening, instead repeatedly stating that she stood to wave after the girls went missing for fifty years.

The girls are stunned at the revelation, realizing they crossed a border between two time periods, just as HIM's laughter emerges. As he explains what happened, wisps of smoke begin emerging from the ground as Blossom sweats, obviously under pressure and as they glance around, the smoke appears from behind Blossom while HIM soon emerges, gleefully telling them that seconds, minutes, hours, days, and nights crawled by on hands and knees as they raced the speed of light. HIM then appears and floats up past them. Sensing something behind them, Blossom and Buttercup turn around while Bubbles points at HIM who is now sitting on the edge of a cliff, asking Buttercup if she remembers.

Suddenly, Buttercup hears herself arrogantly challenging her sisters to a race and as this happens, she clutches her head in dismay, saying, "No!" HIM gleefully agrees and soon gets up from where he was sitting before stating that as the girls traveled through time, the whole world went to his dimension. Blossom urges her sisters not to believe the lie and they all take off, approaching HIM who simply lets out an evil laugh.

For the next few minutes, the girls channeling all their anger, rage, and grief into their attacks proceed to beat HIM and after silently crying, they give one final punch which sends HIM crashing to the ground. HIM, however, recovers quickly and snaps his claws just as the girls descend on him again. As they continue fighting HIM, it then shows various newspaper headings hanging on the walls with some detailing the Powerpuff Girls' disappearance, the Professor being exposed as a fraud, the Mayor presumably dying at the age of 83, and "Who is HIM?". Once the fight with HIM ended, HIM, having recovered at a frighteningly fast level, mockingly asks the girls if they're finished. But, Buttercup assures HIM that he is while Blossom tells him that he can never beat them.

Him's true demonic form.
It is soon clear that the usual fighting has taken its toll on the girls as they're all breathing rather heavily and sweating too. HIM then begins to mockingly say if they've beaten him before he remarks with his eyes now glowing bright green that he's already won. As he says those words, HIM promptly launches himself in the air before his whole body soon grows bigger and more muscular than ever before a jolt of lighting erupts. As the lighting ends, it is shown that HIM now has grown into a powerful monster nearly hundred times the size he normally is. He then gleefully remarks in a demonic voice that the beauty lies in the blame because it is their fault for leaving, before stating, "Just ask your friends."

It then cuts to show of a group of aged and broken children who approach the girls and tell them that they did that, since the children think that the girls had become traitors. While Bubbles puts her hands to her mouth, stunned, and a distressed Blossom says "No.", but the monstrous HIM states to the girls that all he did was take over and it was easy. Then, while descending on the girls, the aged children ask them why they have left them and tell the girls that they were not here to protect the children and it is their fault. After that, the children begin chanting, "Your fault.", while pointing to the girls, with the accusations leaving them deeply guilt-ridden and distressed. As the chanting goes on, Blossom, after believing that the monstrous HIM is right, brokenly states, "What have we done?" before he just does an evil laugh demonically. The chanting continues as both Buttercup and Bubbles struggle to cope with Buttercup yelling "No! No! No!" while Bubbles starts crying out "WHYYY?!?!" and pulling her pigtails in distress. As the townspeople continue chanting, the girls soon rush up into space, screaming before making a circle for a few seconds. Then, as one, they then fly down to the planet, going back the way they came as they head back into the present, having seen the horrific future.

Once they've emerged, the girls then crash-land into some grass and after recovering, realize with joy that Townsville and its people are all intact and alive. As they head home, the girls say hi to the male passerby who's now walking his dog, Ms. Keane, the Mayor, and Ms. Bellum. Once they see the Professor, they crash into him and while embracing him, begin talking about what happened. The Professor is stunned but tells the girls they've got to get ready for their vacation. However, after realizing what would happen in their absence, they tell the Professor that they cannot leave Townsville, not even for a weekend away in the Bahamas.

The episode ends with the Narrator fooling the viewers into believing that he was also affected with the time travel aging, and after the standard end phrase, he says he will never drive over 55 mph again.
